---
title: "Wildfire data from the ECMWF Atmosphere Data Store (ADS)"
format: gfm
  # docx:
  #   toc: true
  #   number-sections: true
  #   highlight-style: github
editor: source
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

```

To reproduce/run for own dowmin, must [register (for free!) with ecmwf](https://accounts.ecmwf.int/auth/realms/ecmwf/login-actions/registration?client_id=cds&tab_id=kWV0xKsufvs) get the API key after registration
Open 01_download.R and drop in the API key, running the script will download netcdf (.nc files) to the data folder
02_brick.R converts each one to raster bricks

03_plot.R stacks all the bricks

uses the function app in terra and base which.max to calculate the hour when each cell experienced the highest concentration. 

![](plots/which_max.png)

Plots a histogram of the frequencies from this

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE}

bs <- list.files("bricks", "pmwf_", full.names = TRUE)

b <- rast(bs)

# for each cell find the hour when the concentration was the highest
wm <- app(b,which.max)

# plot frequency of this
hist(wm, main = "Raster Value Distribution")

```

Top 20 hours

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE}

# sort descending
freq_table <- freq(wm) |> 
  select(hour = value, count) |> 
  arrange(desc(count)) |> 
  slice(1:20)

kable(freq_table)


```

Based on this can pick a period of interest.


And plot it as an animation

![](plots/wildfires.gif)
